sound_type: low grunts display -- low, guttural 'ik-ik-ik' grunts during head-throw courtship display. 200-1800 Hz. E=3. Close-range courtship signal. Female: soft harsh 'crrr' growling purr. The aristocrat communicates with restraint.
ecosystem: Marsh lake -- breeds on prairie pothole wetlands and boreal marshes across western North America. Winters on large lakes, bays, and coastal waters. Deep-water diver feeding on wild celery (Vallisneria) and other aquatic plants.
season: Winter visitor to PNW. Breeds on prairie pothole wetlands (Dakotas, Montana, Saskatchewan, Alberta). Small numbers winter on Puget Sound and freshwater lakes. Arrives fall, departs spring.
family: Anatidae (9th consecutive: 118-126 -- TIES PICIDAE NONET ALL-TIME RECORD)

coordinates.description: Reference for winter habitat. Canvasback winters in small numbers on Puget Sound and associated freshwater lakes. Prefers freshwater and brackish water with submerged vegetation.

taxonomy_note.description: Ninth consecutive Anatidae (118-126). TIES the Picidae nonet ALL-TIME RECORD (9 consecutive, degrees 107-115). Second consecutive Aythya (Greater Scaup 125, Canvasback 126). The 'aristocrat of ducks' -- North America's fastest-flying duck (72 mph). Species name valisineria from Vallisneria (wild celery), the preferred food. 'Canvasback' from the canvas-colored back plumage.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Anatidae: 14+ entries across the engine. Nine consecutive at degrees 118-126. TIES Picidae nonet record.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Aythya from Greek aithuia (a seabird). valisineria from Vallisneria, the wild celery plant genus -- the Canvasback's preferred food and the source of its prized flavor. 'Canvasback' from the pale, canvas-colored back plumage of the male.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Aythya -- second entry. Greater Scaup (125) and Canvasback (126). Bay ducks / pochards. Deep-water divers with different breeding strategies: Scaup on subarctic tundra, Canvasback on prairie pothole marshes.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Anseriformes. Ninth consecutive Anatidae. TIES ALL-TIME RECORD.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Anatidae at ninth consecutive. TIES Picidae nonet ALL-TIME RECORD. Degree 127 determines whether the record is broken (10th Anatidae) or remains tied.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 200-1,800 Hz. Low-frequency grunts. Below Greater Scaup (500-2,500 Hz) and well below Common Goldeneye wing-whistle (1,000-4,000 Hz).
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 300-1,200 Hz. Peak energy in the low-mid range.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Male courtship grunts: 200-1,800 Hz. Female growl: 300-1,500 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Brief. Series of 2-5 grunts: each 0.1-0.2 seconds. Full series: 0.5-1.0 seconds. Not sustained.
song_metrics.note_structure: LOW GUTTURAL GRUNTS: Percussive onset, rapid decay. 'ik-ik-ik' during head-throw display. Deep, short, repeated in series. Accompanying visual display is dramatic; the sound is quiet.
song_metrics.note_types: Male courtship grunt (primary, head-throw display), female growl/purr (alarm/disturbance), female croak (flight call, rare).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Series of 2-5 grunts during head-throw. No sustained phrases. Brief, discrete courtship vocalizations.
song_metrics.timbre: Guttural. Deep. Percussive onset. Organic quality -- not nasal (like scaup) or mechanical (like goldeneye). The grunt has a raw, throaty character.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=3. Low. Audible at 10-25 meters. Close-range courtship signal. The head-throw display is visually dramatic but acoustically quiet.
song_metrics.tempo: Rapid series: 2-5 grunts in 0.5-1.0 seconds. Given during discrete display events, not continuously.
song_metrics.context: COURTSHIP DISPLAY primarily. The head-throw display with grunts is given by males on the water during breeding season. Female growl given during incubation disturbance. Both are close-range, context-specific.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Simple vocalization with little learned variation. Consistent across North American range.
song_metrics.key_property: LOW GRUNTS DISPLAY -- guttural 'ik-ik-ik' during head-throw courtship. E=3. 200-1,800 Hz. Close-range. The aristocrat communicates with restraint. NINTH CONSECUTIVE ANATIDAE (118-126) -- TIES PICIDAE NONET ALL-TIME RECORD. North America's fastest duck (72 mph). The sloping forehead-to-bill profile (diagnostic). Canvas-colored back. Wild celery diet (Vallisneria). 600,000-700,000 globally. Prairie pothole breeder. Bellwether species.

plumage.overall: Male: rich red-brown head, canvas-colored (pale whitish gray) back, black breast, white flanks. Female: warm brown overall, paler on flanks, slightly rufous head. Both sexes: distinctive sloping forehead-to-bill profile.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: SLOPING PROFILE. Smooth concave slope from crown to bill tip. No other North American duck has this shape. Visible at long range and in silhouette. Male: canvas-colored back (paler than Redhead's gray). Richer red head than Redhead.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Eclipse plumage in summer -- males duller but retain the diagnostic sloping profile. Non-breeding plumage less vivid.
plumage.flight_pattern: Fast, direct, powerful flight. 72 mph -- North America's fastest duck. Pointed wings, streamlined body. Aerodynamic head profile contributes to speed.

physical_description.overall: Large diving duck. Body length 48-61 cm. Wingspan 79-89 cm. Weight 860-1600 g. Larger than Greater Scaup, similar to Redhead but with diagnostic profile.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Sloping forehead-to-bill profile. Canvas-colored back. Long bill. In flight: fast, powerful, with pointed wings. The profile is visible at any range where head shape is discernible.
physical_description.field_id: The sloping profile is definitive. If the forehead slopes smoothly into the bill (concave curve), it is a Canvasback. If the forehead meets the bill at an angle (rounded head), it is a Redhead. Male: canvas-pale back (not gray). Richer red head. Longer bill. In flight: fastest duck, powerful wingbeats.

nodes.title: Ninth Consecutive Anatidae -- TIES ALL-TIME RECORD
nodes.description: Nine consecutive Anatidae (118-126). TIES the Picidae nonet (107-115) as the longest family streak in engine history. Four genera, nine ecological niches, nine acoustic profiles. Degree 127 determines whether the record is broken or remains tied.

nodes.title: The Aristocrat of Ducks -- Speed, Elegance, Prestige
nodes.description: North America's fastest duck (72 mph). The sloping forehead-to-bill profile. Canvas-colored back. Prized for speed, difficulty, and wild celery flavor. 19th-century designation. The most prestigious North American diving duck.

nodes.title: The Sloping Profile -- Aerodynamic Field Mark
nodes.description: Smooth concave slope from crown to bill tip. Functionally aerodynamic (contributes to 72 mph speed). Definitive field mark vs. Redhead (rounded profile). Visible at range and in silhouette. No other N. American duck has this shape.

nodes.title: Canvasback vs. Redhead Identification
nodes.description: Both Aythya with reddish heads. Canvasback: sloping profile, longer bill, paler back, richer red. Redhead: rounded head, shorter bill, darker gray back. Profile difference is definitive.

nodes.title: Aythya Pair Complete (125-126)
nodes.description: Greater Scaup (125, nasal call, bay rafter, tundra breeder) and Canvasback (126, guttural grunts, prairie breeder). Same genus, same E=3, different acoustic character. Both deep-water divers, different breeding strategies.

nodes.title: Anatidae Nonet Diversity (118-126)
nodes.description: Four genera (Histrionicus, Mergus/Lophodytes, Aix, Bucephala, Aythya). Stream to prairie to bay. Fish-eaters to plant-eaters. Cavity nesters to ground nesters. E=3-5. Nine entries, nine distinct niches. No repetition.

cross_references.relationship: AYTHYA PAIR. Second consecutive Aythya. Scaup (nasal call, bay rafter, tundra breeder, E=3) and Canvasback (guttural grunts, prairie breeder, E=3). Same genus, same energy, different vocal character.
ANATIDAE NONET COMPLETE! Nine consecutive (118-126). Harlequin started the streak at E=5. Canvasback completes the nonet at E=3. TIES Picidae all-time record (107-115). Stream to prairie.
RECORD TIE! Anatidae nonet (118-126) ties Picidae nonet (107-115). Two families, two streaks, nine consecutive each. Picidae: E=2-6. Anatidae: E=3-5. Co-holders of the all-time record.
ANATIDAE DIVERSITY. Wood Duck (cavity nester, forest pools, E=3) and Canvasback (ground nester, prairie marshes, E=3). Same family, same energy, completely different ecology. The streak's range.

SPEED COMPARISON. Common Goldeneye: fast flier, speed produces sound (wing-whistle, E=4). Canvasback: fastest duck (72 mph), speed is silent (no wing-whistle). Speed with sound vs. speed with silence.
